summary: Raw sources preserve original context. Compiled Wiki notes turn useful source-backed claims into concise, reusable knowledge. - Raw notes keep evidence intact. - Compiled notes reduce repeated broad-context reading. - Source links keep compiled claims traceable back to the original material.
title: Raw Vs Compiled Knowledge

summary: The starter demo source was compiled into initial topic, concept, project, and log notes. - Raw source: `Raw/Sources/llm-wiki-starter-demo-source.md` - Topic note: `Wiki/Topics/llm-wiki-workflow.md` - Concept note: `Wiki/Concepts/raw-vs-compiled-knowledge.md` - Project note: `Wiki/Projects/build-core-llm-wiki.md`
title: Initial Demo Ingest

summary: Build a basic LLM Wiki where sources live in `Raw/Sources/` and reusable compiled knowledge lives under `Wiki/`. - The demo source has been captured in the Raw layer. - Initial compiled notes show the intended transformation from source material to reusable Wiki knowledge. - Add real source notes to `Raw/Sources/`. - Search the compiled catalog before opening broad Raw context. - Keep future compiled notes linked to
title: Build Core LLM Wiki

summary: An LLM Wiki workflow separates captured source material from compiled notes so future agents can query concise knowledge before opening broad source context. - Preserve source material in `Raw/Sources/`. - Compile useful claims into short notes under `Wiki/`. - Search compiled Wiki notes first. - Open Raw sources only when more evidence or detail is needed. - [[raw-vs-compiled-knowledge]] - [[build-core-llm-wiki]]
title: LLM Wiki Workflow
